subject: current-affairs
description: Pahalgam Attack & India Pakistan Crisis 2025 MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Militants opened fire on a group of tourists at Pahalgam in Indian Illegally Occupied Jammu & Kashmir on:
mcqs.explanation: The Pahalgam terror attack took place on 22 April 2025 in the Baisaran Valley of IIOJK.

mcqs.q: How many people were killed in the 2025 Pahalgam attack?
mcqs.explanation: 26 people — mostly tourists — were killed in the Pahalgam terror attack on 22 April 2025.

mcqs.q: Pahalgam is located in which valley of Jammu & Kashmir?
mcqs.explanation: Pahalgam is located in the Baisaran Valley, a tourist meadow in Indian-administered Kashmir.

mcqs.q: Pakistan suspended visas for Indian nationals and closed its airspace to Indian aircraft in retaliation for the Pahalgam accusations on:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan formally announced visa suspension and airspace closure for Indian carriers on 24 April 2025.

mcqs.q: After the Pahalgam attack, India suspended which bilateral treaty?
mcqs.explanation: India suspended the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan — the first suspension since the treaty was signed in 1960.

mcqs.q: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed between Pakistan and India in:
mcqs.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960 under the mediation of the World Bank, allocating the waters of the Indus river system between India and Pakistan.

mcqs.q: All madrasas in Azad Kashmir were ordered closed for 10 days citing tensions with India on:
mcqs.explanation: AJK authorities ordered the 10-day closure of madrasas on 1 May 2025 as a safety precaution.

mcqs.q: On 3 May 2025, the Pakistani military test-fired which ballistic missile?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan test-fired the Abdali short-range ballistic missile on 3 May 2025.

mcqs.q: On 5 May 2025 Pakistan test-fired the Fateh missile. The Fateh series is a:
mcqs.explanation: The Fateh missile is a Pakistani surface-to-surface guided multi-launch rocket / ballistic weapon system.

mcqs.q: On which date did India launch missile strikes into Pakistani territory in response to the 2025 Pahalgam attack?
mcqs.explanation: India launched Operation Sindoor on 7 May 2025 — striking targets in Azad Kashmir and Pakistani Punjab.

mcqs.q: On which date did the Pakistan Cricket Board transfer the remaining matches of PSL 2025 to the UAE?
mcqs.explanation: After an Indian drone landed near Rawalpindi Cricket Stadium, the PCB transferred remaining PSL 2025 matches to the UAE on 9 May 2025.

mcqs.q: The Pakistan Cricket Board ordered the transfer of the remaining eight matches of PSL 2025 to which country?
mcqs.explanation: The remaining eight matches of PSL 2025 were shifted to the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

mcqs.q: PSL 2025 resumed in Pakistan on:
mcqs.explanation: Following the 10 May ceasefire, PSL 2025 resumed in Pakistan on 17 May 2025.

mcqs.q: India and Pakistan reached a ceasefire agreement on:
mcqs.explanation: The India–Pakistan ceasefire agreement was reached on 10 May 2025, announced publicly by US President Donald Trump.

mcqs.q: After the intervention of which country did India and Pakistan agree on a ceasefire in May 2025?
mcqs.explanation: The US (Trump administration) played the primary mediating role that produced the 10 May 2025 India–Pakistan ceasefire.

mcqs.q: General Asim Munir, Chief of the Army Staff of the Pakistan Army, was promoted to Field Marshal on:
mcqs.explanation: General Syed Asim Munir was promoted to Field Marshal on 20 May 2025.

mcqs.q: General Asim Munir is the second Pakistani to receive the title of Field Marshal. The first Pakistani Field Marshal was:
mcqs.explanation: General Ayub Khan was Pakistan's first Field Marshal, promoted in 1959 when he was also President.

mcqs.q: On which date did India issue an official flood advisory to Pakistan — the first formal contact since the 2025 crisis?
mcqs.explanation: India issued an official flood advisory to Pakistan on 25 August 2025 — the first formal contact between the two countries after the May 2025 military crisis.

mcqs.q: India's missile-strike operation launched on 7 May 2025 against Pakistan and AJK was codenamed:
mcqs.explanation: India codenamed its 7 May 2025 operation 'Operation Sindoor'.

mcqs.q: The first drone battle between the nuclear-armed neighbours India and Pakistan began on:
mcqs.explanation: The first large-scale drone battle between India and Pakistan began on 7 May 2025, alongside India's Operation Sindoor missile strikes.

mcqs.q: In the early hours of 10 May, India accused Pakistan of launching missile attacks on Indian air bases including:
mcqs.explanation: Reported Indian air-base targets on 10 May 2025 included Udhampur, Pathankot, Adampur and Sirsa — i.e. all of these.

mcqs.q: Pakistan accused India of launching attacks on several Pakistan air bases including:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan accused India of targeting Nur Khan (Chaklala), Rafiqi and Murid air bases — i.e. all of these.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's operation targeting Indian military bases was codenamed:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan codenamed its military response 'Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos' — drawn from Surah As-Saff 61:4.

mcqs.q: Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos was launched on:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan launched Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos on 10 May 2025.

mcqs.q: The May 2025 India–Pakistan ceasefire was formalised through a hotline communication between the two countries' DGMOs on:
mcqs.explanation: After four days of escalation, the ceasefire was agreed via a DGMO hotline communication on 10 May 2025.

mcqs.q: The Indus Waters Treaty was originally mediated by which international institution?
mcqs.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty (1960) was brokered under the mediation of the World Bank.

mcqs.q: Nur Khan air base — reportedly targeted during the 2025 crisis — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Nur Khan air base is in Chaklala, Rawalpindi — adjacent to Islamabad.

mcqs.q: Pahalgam is popularly known as which kind of tourist destination?
mcqs.explanation: Pahalgam is a popular hill station and meadow destination in the Kashmir valley — the Baisaran valley near it is nicknamed 'Mini Switzerland'.

mcqs.q: What does 'Bunyan Al Marsoos' mean in Arabic?
options: A solid structure firmly joined together
mcqs.explanation: 'Bunyan Al Marsoos' literally means 'a solid structure firmly joined together' — a Quranic metaphor for believers' unity.

mcqs.q: The Quranic phrase 'bunyan al marsoos' appears in:
mcqs.explanation: The phrase appears in Surah As-Saff (Chapter 61), verse 4.

mcqs.q: 'DGMO' — the officials who formalised the 10 May 2025 ceasefire — stands for:
mcqs.explanation: DGMO stands for Director General of Military Operations — the top operational military commander in each country.

mcqs.q: The promotion of General Asim Munir to Field Marshal was announced by the federal cabinet under Prime Minister:
mcqs.explanation: PM Shehbaz Sharif's federal cabinet approved General Asim Munir's promotion to Field Marshal on 20 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Pakistan closed its airspace to Indian aircraft during the 2025 crisis primarily to:
options: Cut a vital India-Europe overflight route
mcqs.explanation: Closing its airspace cut off a critical route used by Indian airlines for westbound (India-Europe / India-West) overflights, imposing significant commercial and diplomatic costs on India.

mcqs.q: The Fateh surface-to-surface missile is primarily classified as a:
mcqs.explanation: The Fateh missile series is a short-range guided multi-launch rocket / ballistic system, used by the Pakistan Army.

mcqs.q: The May 2025 India–Pakistan crisis is often called:
mcqs.explanation: The four-day escalation from 7 to 10 May 2025 is commonly referred to as the '2025 India–Pakistan Crisis' or 'Four-Day Conflict' in international and Pakistani media.
